name: Douala Maritime Museum
description: Explore the rich maritime and trade history of the Cameroonian coast.

name: La Nouvelle Liberté Statue
description: A striking contemporary monument constructed from recycled metal in the heart of Douala.

name: Sanaga River Bridge
description: A historic colonial-era bridge crossing Cameroon's largest river at Edea.

name: Ekom-Nkam Waterfalls
description: Spectacular 80m twin waterfalls featured in the Greystoke Tarzan movie. A true hidden gem of the Littoral.

name: Manoka Island
description: A serene island featuring colonial remnants, monkey reserves, and mangrove forests just off Douala.

name: National Museum of Yaoundé
description: Housed in the former presidential palace, showcasing Cameroonian art and history.

name: Mvog-Betsi Zoo
description: A botanical and zoological garden home to primates and big cats in the capital.

name: Monument de la Réunification
description: Iconic spiraling monument symbolizing the reunification of French and British Cameroon.

name: Ebogo Eco-Tourism Site
description: Canoe rides on the Nyong river, giant trees, and forest walks in a serene eco-paradise near Mbalmayo.

name: Nachtigal Falls
description: Beautiful rapids and waterfalls along the Sanaga river near Bafia.

name: Bafoussam Chiefdom
description: A highly organized traditional kingdom with stunning Bamileke architecture and ceremonial rites.

name: Mount Bamboutos
description: The third highest peak in Cameroon, offering phenomenal hiking trails and breathtaking views from Dschang.

name: Foumban Royal Palace
description: An incredible architectural marvel representing the deeply historic Bamoun Kingdom.

name: Sultan's Museum
description: Contains thousands of artifacts detailing the Bamoun dynasty and its unique syllabic alphabet.

name: Metche Waterfalls
description: A sacred waterfall serving as a traditional site of purification and sacrifice near Bafoussam.

name: Mankon Museum
description: A rich museum preserving the artisanal crafts and history of the Mankon kingdom in Bamenda.

name: Lake Awing
description: A mystical high-altitude crater lake surrounded by sacred forests near Bamenda.

name: Mount Oku
description: The second highest mountain in mainland West Africa, known for its endemic bird species and crater lake.

name: Menchum Falls
description: A breathtaking cascading waterfall just off the main road near Wum.

name: Bafut Palace
description: One of the oldest traditional palaces, featuring the impressive Achum shrine building near Bamenda.

name: Mount Cameroon
description: One of Africa's largest active volcanoes and highest peak in West Africa, attracting hikers from across the globe.

name: Limbe Wildlife Centre
description: A sanctuary rescuing and rehabilitating primates, including gorillas and chimpanzees, in the heart of Limbe.

name: Limbe Botanic Garden
description: Established in 1892, featuring incredible tropical plant biodiversity and historical colonial graves.

name: Korup National Park
description: Africa's oldest and most biologically diverse rainforest, accessible via a suspension bridge from Kumba.

name: Down Beach
description: Famed for its volcanic black sand and freshly grilled seafood straight from the Atlantic ocean.

name: Waza National Park
description: A huge UNESCO biosphere reserve packed with lions, elephants, giraffes, and birdlife near Maroua.

name: Mandara Mountains
description: Rugged volcanic peaks jutting out of the dry plains near Mokolo, dotted with traditional Mafa villages.

name: Rhumsiki Peaks
description: The famous volcanic needle rock formations near Mokolo, offering the most spectacular sunsets in the Sahel.

name: Lake Chad Shores
description: The marshy expanses of the historic shrinking lake near Yagoua, vital for nomadic herders and fishers.

name: Maroua Artisanal Center
description: A massive market in Maroua famous for intricate leatherwork, pottery, and vibrant Sahel fabrics.

name: Benoue National Park
description: A large wildlife reserve near Garoua featuring the Benoue river, hippos, and the Lord Derby eland.

name: Faro National Park
description: A remote, dense savanna park home to elephants, cheetahs, and rhinos, reachable from Garoua.

name: Kola Gorges
description: Spectacular narrow, deep canyons carved into the rock by rivers over millennia, near Guider.

name: Lagdo Dam
description: An enormous hydroelectric dam and scenic reservoir on the Benue river near Garoua.

name: Bidzar Petroglyphs
description: Ancient geometric rock engravings dating back thousands of years, discovered near Figuil.

name: Tello Waterfalls
description: A gorgeous wide waterfall cascading over a basalt rock cave near Ngaoundéré.

name: Lake Dang
description: A peaceful crater lake located next to the University of Ngaoundéré — perfect for picnics.

name: Vina River Falls
description: A dramatically scenic canyon waterfall where the Vina river drops into a wide basin near Ngaoundéré.

name: Mount Ngaoundéré
description: Overlooking Ngaoundéré city, providing panoramic views across the entire Adamawa plateau.

name: Banyo Lamidat
description: The impressive traditional palace of the Banyo Lamido — a powerful and historic local ruler.

name: Lobéké National Park
description: Dense tropical rainforest near Batouri, famous for its forest elephant clearings called bais.

name: Dja Faunal Reserve
description: A UNESCO World Heritage site and one of Africa's largest and best-protected equatorial rainforests.

name: Boumba Bek National Park
description: A remote park highly protected and packed with western lowland gorillas and chimpanzees near Bertoua.

name: Deng Deng National Park
description: A crucial sanctuary protecting the northernmost population of Western Lowland Gorillas.

name: Nki National Park
description: Features massive waterfalls and pristine, unexplored river systems deep in the eastern jungle.

name: Lobe Waterfalls
description: The world-famous waterfalls that drop dramatically straight into the Atlantic Ocean near Kribi.

name: Campo Ma'an National Park
description: Home to gorillas and elephants that occasionally wander down to the Kribi beaches.

name: Grand Batanga Beach
description: Pristine, quiet stretch of golden sandy beaches perfect for swimming and relaxing near Kribi.

name: Kribi Lighthouse
description: A historic German colonial lighthouse built in 1906, standing sentinel over the Kribi harbor.

name: Nkolandom Tourist Center
description: A beautifully designed eco-village near Ebolowa showcasing rock formations, museum, and forest walks.
